The **Copper Box Arena** is a multi-sport venue built for the [2012 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2012_Summer_Olympics "2012 Summer Olympics"), located in the [Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_Olympic_Park "Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park") in [London](/wiki/London "London"), England.

Previously known as the **Handball Arena**, it was renamed because, aside from [handball](/wiki/Handball_at_the_2012_Summer_Olympics "Handball at the 2012 Summer Olympics"), it hosted [modern pentathlon](/wiki/Modern_pentathlon_at_the_2012_Summer_Olympics "Modern pentathlon at the 2012 Summer Olympics") (fencing, shooting, swimming, horse jumping and running) during the Olympics and was the [goalball](/wiki/Goalball_at_the_2012_Summer_Paralympics "Goalball at the 2012 Summer Paralympics") venue for the [2012 Summer Paralympics](/wiki/2012_Summer_Paralympics "2012 Summer Paralympics").[[4]](#cite_note-anger-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copper_Box_Arena&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

London's Olympic bid proposed that there would be four indoor arenas in the Olympic Park, in addition to other main venues, but the revised masterplan published in 2006 reduced this to three, with the volleyball being moved to [Earls Court Exhibition Centre](/wiki/Earls_Court_Exhibition_Centre "Earls Court Exhibition Centre").[[5]](#cite_note-5) The fencing arena was also cancelled, and the fencing took place at [ExCeL](/wiki/ExCeL "ExCeL"). Construction of the building was completed on time in early 2011 and came in under budget.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The design incorporates [light pipes](/wiki/Light_tube "Light tube") and [rainwater collectors](/wiki/Rainwater_harvesting "Rainwater harvesting") to reduce both energy and water use by 40%.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Test events were successfully hosted at the venue ahead of the Olympic and Paralympic Games in 2012.

In January 2012, the venue was renamed the Copper Box from the Handball Arena, thus reflecting the look of the building and the fact that it would not just be used for Handball.[[8]](#cite_note-8) [British Handball](/wiki/British_Handball "British Handball") campaigned for the rebrand to be reversed.[[4]](#cite_note-anger-4)

## Olympics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copper_Box_Arena&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Olympics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/Copper_Box_interior.JPG/250px-Copper_Box_interior.JPG)](/wiki/File:Copper_Box_interior.JPG)

The Copper Box interior before a handball game

The Copper Box has 7,000 seats. It was used for [handball](/wiki/Team_handball "Team handball") preliminaries and [modern pentathlon](/wiki/Modern_pentathlon "Modern pentathlon") fencing during the Olympic Games, and for [goalball](/wiki/Goalball "Goalball") during the [Paralympic Games](/wiki/2012_Paralympic_Games "2012 Paralympic Games"). The handball men's quarter-finals, all semi-finals and both finals were held at the larger [Basketball Arena](/wiki/Basketball_Arena_(London) "Basketball Arena (London)").

The Copper Box was praised for its loud, exciting atmosphere during handball games.[[9]](#cite_note-9) For the Paralympics goalball events the crowd in the Box were required to be quiet.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Post-Olympics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Copper_Box_Arena&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Post-Olympics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3d/Copper_Box%2C_16_April_2012.jpg/250px-Copper_Box%2C_16_April_2012.jpg)](/wiki/File:Copper_Box,_16_April_2012.jpg)

Aerial view of the building in 2012

The venue has been adapted into a multi-sport arena for community use, athlete training and major events.

[British Basketball League](/wiki/British_Basketball_League "British Basketball League") side [London Lions](/wiki/London_Lions_(basketball) "London Lions (basketball)") relocated to the Copper Box for the [2013–14 season](/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_British_Basketball_League_season "2013–14 British Basketball League season").[[11]](#cite_note-11) They played their first game there on 14 August 2013 in front of a sell-out crowd, losing 72–107 to the [Iowa Hawkeyes](/wiki/Iowa_Hawkeyes_men%27s_basketball "Iowa Hawkeyes men's basketball") American college basketball team in an exhibition match.[[12]](#cite_note-12) The [Lions' women's team](/wiki/London_Lions_(women) "London Lions (women)"), playing in the [WBBL](/wiki/Women%27s_British_Basketball_League "Women's British Basketball League"), moved to the venue in 2017.

The venue played host to the [2015 Netball Superleague Grand Final](/wiki/2015_Netball_Superleague_Grand_Final "2015 Netball Superleague Grand Final"),[[13]](#cite_note-13) which saw the [Surrey Storm](/wiki/Surrey_Storm "Surrey Storm") win their first Netball Superleague title, defeating the [Hertfordshire Mavericks](/wiki/Hertfordshire_Mavericks "Hertfordshire Mavericks"), 56–39. The venue is the home arena for the [London Pulse](/wiki/London_Pulse "London Pulse"), who since 2018 have played in the Superleague. It has also hosted several home test matches for the [England national team](/wiki/England_national_netball_team "England national netball team").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The Copper Box hosted the [London Grand Prix](/wiki/London_Grand_Prix_Gold "London Grand Prix Gold") [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") tournament on 1–6 October 2013. The London Grand Prix forms part of the [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation")'s Grand Prix Gold series.

The [Wheelchair Rugby](/wiki/Wheelchair_Rugby "Wheelchair Rugby") event at the 2014 [Invictus Games](/wiki/Invictus_Games_(multi-sport_event) "Invictus Games (multi-sport event)") was held at the venue in September 2014, as was the BT World Wheelchair Rugby Challenge in October 2015.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[London GD Handball Club](/wiki/London_GD_Handball_Club "London GD Handball Club"), one of the oldest handball clubs in the country, used the venue for its home matches.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The floor was badly damaged during a mass fitness event in January 2014, resulting in several netball internationals having to be played elsewhere at the last minute.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

*[Counter-Strike: Global Offensive](/wiki/Counter-Strike:_Global_Offensive "Counter-Strike: Global Offensive")* and *[Super Smash Bros. Melee](/wiki/Super_Smash_Bros._Melee "Super Smash Bros. Melee")* [DreamHack](/wiki/DreamHack "DreamHack") London 2015 took place at the Copper Box on 19–20 September.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

On 3 February 2018, the Copper Box hosted the amateur boxing fight between [YouTubers](/wiki/List_of_YouTubers "List of YouTubers") [KSI and Joe Weller](/wiki/KSI_vs._Joe_Weller "KSI vs. Joe Weller").[[17]](#cite_note-17) It has also hosted [IBF](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") boxing.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

From 22 to 25 February 2018, the venue played host to the [ITTF Team World Cup](/wiki/2018_ITTF_Team_World_Cup "2018 ITTF Team World Cup") table tennis competition.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

The [Rocket League Championship Series](/wiki/Rocket_League_Championship_Series "Rocket League Championship Series") Season 5 World Championship, an Esports event sponsored by [Psyonix](/wiki/Psyonix "Psyonix"), the video game company who created *[Rocket League](/wiki/Rocket_League "Rocket League")*, was hosted there on 8–10 June 2018.[[20]](#cite_note-20) The event was notable for "The Shot", in which Justin "jstn." Morales, a player on [NRG](/wiki/NRG_Esports "NRG Esports"), scored a last-second goal that forced overtime in game 7 of the World Championship Grand Final Bracket Reset. The RLCS would return to the Copper Box for the 2021–2022 season Spring Major on 29 June–3 July 2022, with crowds only being present on the final two days. [Moist Esports](/wiki/Moist_Esports "Moist Esports") won that Major's Grand Final 4–3 in series 1 and 4–0 in the Bracket Reset.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Great Britain played Kazakhstan at the arena in April 2019, in the tennis [Fed Cup World Group II play-offs](/wiki/2019_Fed_Cup_World_Group_II_Play-offs "2019 Fed Cup World Group II Play-offs").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

On 31 August 2019, the venue was used for [New Japan Pro-Wrestling](/wiki/New_Japan_Pro-Wrestling "New Japan Pro-Wrestling")'s show in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), [Royal Quest](/wiki/Royal_Quest_(2019) "Royal Quest (2019)").[[23]](#cite_note-23) NJPW returned to the Copper Box for [Royal Quest III](/wiki/Royal_Quest_III "Royal Quest III") on 13 October 2023.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

From 14 to 16 November 2019 the venue hosted [X0](/wiki/X_(Xbox_show) "X (Xbox show)"), an annual event presented by [Xbox](/wiki/Xbox "Xbox").[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Between January and July 2020, the Copper Box Arena was used as the home venue for the [London Royal Ravens](/wiki/London_Royal_Ravens "London Royal Ravens"), an [eSports](/wiki/ESports "ESports") team taking part in the inaugural [Call of Duty League](/wiki/Call_of_Duty_League "Call of Duty League").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The [Apex Legends Global Series](/wiki/Apex_Legends_Global_Series "Apex Legends Global Series") Year 3 Split 1 finals, was hosted there on 2-5 Feb 2023.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

The arena was one of the three venues selected to host matches in the [2021 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup](/wiki/2021_Wheelchair_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2021 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

The arena hosted the [League of Legends MSI 2023 tournament](/wiki/2023_Mid-Season_Invitational "2023 Mid-Season Invitational") between 2-21 May 2023.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

[Revolution Pro Wrestling](/wiki/Revolution_Pro_Wrestling "Revolution Pro Wrestling") (Otherwise known as RevPro) hosted their [11th](/wiki/RevPro_11th_Anniversary_Show "RevPro 11th Anniversary Show") and [12th anniversary shows](/wiki/RevPro_12th_Anniversary_Show "RevPro 12th Anniversary Show") at the venue on 26 August 2023 and 24 August 2024, each one day before [All Elite Wrestling](/wiki/All_Elite_Wrestling "All Elite Wrestling")'s [All In 2023](/wiki/All_In_(2023) "All In (2023)") and [2024](/wiki/All_In_(2024) "All In (2024)") events at [Wembley Stadium](/wiki/Wembley_Stadium "Wembley Stadium").

In November 2023, the arena was used for the [2023 Billie Jean King Cup](/wiki/2023_Billie_Jean_King_Cup "2023 Billie Jean King Cup") play-offs between [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_Billie_Jean_King_Cup_team "Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup team") and [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden_Billie_Jean_King_Cup_team "Sweden Billie Jean King Cup team"), which determine which team is relegated below the qualifying rounds in 2024.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

On 24 March 2025, the arena hosted the UK's inaugural [Baller League](/wiki/Baller_League "Baller League") fixture.

Beginning in August 2025 the arena hosted the London residency for the band [Gorillaz](/wiki/Gorillaz "Gorillaz").
